content: Just as the double meaning of "reflexion" in language education encompasses both passive reflection of existing knowledge and active metacognitive反思 (self-reflection) to revise writing, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to separate these two distinct modes: a "reflexion" mode that passively mirrors the model’s existing reasoning trajectory, and a separate "reflection" mode that actively critiques that trajectory from inconsistent alternative perspectives. This redefinition unlocks a new framework for improving LLM self-reflection performance.
